--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,176 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: ff8480
+title: Perspective undo doesn't refresh the cache or emit events — Group By (and filter/sort/etc.) undo silently no-ops
+---
+## What
+
+Setting "Group By" on a perspective doesn't undo. The YAML declares `perspective.group` as `undoable: true` (`swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/perspective.yaml:66-75`), and the production wiring correctly registers `PerspectiveStore` with the shared undo stack (`kanban-app/src/state.rs:153-170`, `register_perspective_store`). So the undo entry is present on the stack. What's missing: after the undo reverses the perspective YAML on disk, neither the in-memory `PerspectiveContext` cache nor any frontend listener learns about the change. Result: the perspective still renders grouped, the group popover still shows the pre-undo value, and the user concludes "undo didn't work."
+
+This is the same architectural shape as the earlier board-redraw-after-column-undo bug (`01KPTHBAXKGZGT9DA2E60KT8DK`), applied to perspectives instead of columns. The entity crate has a dedicated post-undo cache-sync hook; perspectives don't.
+
+**Confirmed by tracing**:
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/undo_commands.rs:44-111` — `UndoCmd::execute` calls `StoreContext::undo()` (which rewrites the file on disk), then calls `sync_entity_cache(ctx, &outcome)`, which only knows about `EntityContext::sync_entity_cache_from_disk`. Perspectives don't live in `EntityContext`.
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/mod.rs:256-257` — the generic `swissarmyhammer_entity::UndoCmd` and `RedoCmd` are registered directly as `app.undo` / `app.redo`. There's no kanban-level wrapper that also syncs perspective state.
+- `swissarmyhammer-perspectives/src/context.rs`:
+  - `PerspectiveContext::write` at lines 93-140 emits `PerspectiveEvent::PerspectiveChanged` broadcast (line 131-132) when mutations happen through `.write()`.
+  - **There is no path that emits that event when the file is rewritten by undo.** `PerspectiveStore::undo_erased` rewrites bytes on disk without going through `PerspectiveContext::write`.
+  - No `reload_from_disk` method exists on `PerspectiveContext`; nothing re-reads a single perspective's YAML after an external rewrite.
+- `kanban-app/src/watcher.rs:792-830` — `process_perspective_event` is the bridge that translates `PerspectiveEvent::PerspectiveChanged` into the `entity-field-changed` Tauri event the frontend `perspective-context.tsx` listens for. So IF the event fires, the frontend automatically reacts — the bridge is fine; the bug is that the event never fires on undo.
+
+## Approach
+
+Two parts: (1) teach `PerspectiveContext` to sync its cache and emit the event on post-undo refresh, (2) make `app.undo` / `app.redo` call it when the undo target was a perspective.
+
+### 1. `PerspectiveContext::reload_from_disk`
+
+File: `swissarmyhammer-kanban/swissarmyhammer-perspectives/src/context.rs`
+
+Add an async method that re-reads a single perspective YAML and syncs the in-memory cache + emits the event:
+
+```rust
+/// Refresh a single perspective's in-memory entry from disk.
+///
+/// Used by post-undo / post-redo reconciliation after the store layer has
+/// rewritten the on-disk YAML without going through `write()`. Mirrors the
+/// `EntityContext::sync_entity_cache_from_disk` contract at the entity layer:
+///
+/// - If the file exists and parses, replace the cached entry and emit
+///   `PerspectiveEvent::PerspectiveChanged` so downstream subscribers
+///   (Tauri bridge, frontend refresh) react.
+/// - If the file is absent (undo of a create, or redo of a delete),
+///   evict the cached entry and emit `PerspectiveEvent::PerspectiveDeleted`.
+pub async fn reload_from_disk(&mut self, id: &str) -> Result<()> {
+    let path = self.perspective_path(id);
+    if path.exists() {
+        let content = tokio::fs::read_to_string(&path).await?;
+        let p: Perspective = serde_yaml_ng::from_str(&content)?;
+        // existing id_index/perspectives replace logic — factor from `write`
+        self.replace_or_insert(p.clone());
+        let _ = self.event_sender.send(PerspectiveEvent::PerspectiveChanged {
+            id: p.id.clone(),
+            changed_fields: vec![],  // empty = "all fields may have changed"
+            is_create: false,
+        });
+    } else {
+        self.remove_by_id(id);
+        let _ = self.event_sender.send(PerspectiveEvent::PerspectiveDeleted { id: id.into() });
+    }
+    Ok(())
+}
+```
+
+The `changed_fields: vec![]` signals "unspecified" to the bridge — the frontend's listener refetches the perspective list on any change-notification for `entity_type: "perspective"` (see `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/perspective-context.tsx:160+`), so an empty fields array is the appropriate "full refresh needed" marker.
+
+Factor any existing replace/remove helper from `write` and `delete` so the reload path shares the id-index update logic (don't duplicate).
+
+### 2. Kanban-local `UndoCmd` / `RedoCmd` wrappers
+
+File: new `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/app_undo_commands.rs` (or inline in existing `app_commands.rs` — pick the place that fits the existing layout).
+
+```rust
+pub struct KanbanUndoCmd;
+
+#[async_trait]
+impl Command for KanbanUndoCmd {
+    fn available(&self, ctx: &CommandContext) -> bool {
+        swissarmyhammer_entity::UndoCmd.available(ctx)
+    }
+
+    async fn execute(&self, ctx: &CommandContext) -> Result<Value> {
+        let store_ctx = ctx.require_extension::<StoreContext>()?;
+        let outcome = match store_ctx.undo().await {
+            Ok(o) => o,
+            Err(StoreError::NotFound(_)) => return Ok(json!({"noop": true})),
+            Err(e) => return Err(CommandError::ExecutionFailed(e.to_string())),
+        };
+
+        // Entity cache sync (existing behavior).
+        if let Some(ectx) = ctx.extension::<EntityContext>() {
+            ectx.sync_entity_cache_from_disk(&outcome.store_name, outcome.item_id.as_str()).await;
+        }
+
+        // New: perspective cache sync when the undo targeted a perspective.
+        if outcome.store_name == "perspective" {
+            if let Some(kanban) = ctx.extension::<KanbanContext>() {
+                if let Ok(pctx) = kanban.perspective_context().await {
+                    let mut pctx = pctx.write().await;
+                    if let Err(e) = pctx.reload_from_disk(outcome.item_id.as_str()).await {
+                        tracing::warn!(
+                            id = %outcome.item_id.as_str(),
+                            error = %e,
+                            "perspective reload_from_disk after undo failed"
+                        );
+                    }
+                }
+            }
+        }
+
+        Ok(json!({"undone": true}))
+    }
+}
+
+// KanbanRedoCmd is structurally identical — call store_ctx.redo() and the same sync steps.
+```
+
+File: `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/mod.rs:256-257` — swap registrations:
+```rust
+map.insert("app.undo".into(), Arc::new(app_undo_commands::KanbanUndoCmd));
+map.insert("app.redo".into(), Arc::new(app_undo_commands::KanbanRedoCmd));
+```
+
+The generic `swissarmyhammer_entity::UndoCmd`/`RedoCmd` stay in place for any other crate that mounts an undo surface without perspectives. The kanban-local wrappers are a single 40-line addition each that delegates to the store + adds the perspective sync.
+
+**Why a wrapper instead of a general hook mechanism**: two post-undo reconciliation paths (EntityContext and PerspectiveContext) don't justify a pluggable registry. If a third one appears, refactor then. Two concrete match arms are cheaper to read than a trait with two impls.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] Setting "Group By" on a perspective, then invoking Undo (palette, keyboard, or app-menu), reverts the group field on the perspective. The perspective tab bar's group-by popover shows the pre-undo group value (or cleared state, if there was none).
+- [ ] Setting a filter, then Undo — the filter formula bar shows the pre-undo filter text.
+- [ ] Setting a sort (via `perspective.sort.set` or `perspective.sort.toggle`), then Undo — the column header sort indicator reverts.
+- [ ] Clearing a filter/group/sort, then Undo — the cleared field is restored to its pre-undo value.
+- [ ] Redo restores the post-undo state for each of the above.
+- [ ] Frontend `perspective-context.tsx` refreshes the perspective list via the existing `entity-field-changed` listener (no React changes needed — existing bridge in `watcher.rs:802+` translates `PerspectiveEvent::PerspectiveChanged` → `entity-field-changed`).
+- [ ] No regression in entity-level undo — tasks, tags, columns, attachments still undo and their caches still sync via the entity-cache path.
+- [ ] No duplicate reload: the path that writes through `PerspectiveContext::write` (normal user edits) already emits `PerspectiveChanged` — undo must not emit a second one for the same operation. The only emit comes from the reload hook.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] New Rust unit test in `swissarmyhammer-perspectives/src/context.rs` tests module (alongside the existing `setup_with_undo` helper at lines 386-405):
+  - `reload_from_disk_syncs_cache_and_emits_event_on_file_change`:
+    1. Open context + store handle.
+    2. Write a perspective through the store bypass (e.g. direct file write + manual id-index corruption, or use the store handle's internal write to simulate the undo state).
+    3. Call `reload_from_disk(id)`.
+    4. Assert `get_by_id(id)` returns the new state and the event stream received a `PerspectiveChanged` with `is_create: false`.
+  - `reload_from_disk_evicts_cache_and_emits_deleted_on_file_absence`:
+    1. Populate a perspective.
+    2. Delete the file behind the cache's back.
+    3. Call `reload_from_disk(id)`.
+    4. Assert `get_by_id(id)` returns None and the event stream received `PerspectiveDeleted`.
+- [ ] New Rust integration test `perspective_group_undo_reverts_and_emits_event` in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/undo_cross_cutting.rs`:
+  1. Set up KanbanContext + StoreContext + register perspective store (mirror `kanban-app/src/state.rs:253-257`).
+  2. Create a perspective with `group: None`.
+  3. Dispatch `perspective.group` with `group: "status"`.
+  4. Subscribe to the `PerspectiveEvent` broadcast.
+  5. Dispatch `app.undo`.
+  6. Assert `pctx.get_by_id(id).group == None` — the field reverted in the cache (not just on disk).
+  7. Assert a `PerspectiveChanged` event was received post-undo.
+- [ ] New browser test `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/perspective-context.undo.test.tsx`:
+  1. Mount `PerspectiveProvider` with a mocked Tauri bridge.
+  2. Fire a synthetic `entity-field-changed` event with `entity_type: "perspective"` (simulating what the bridge emits after undo).
+  3. Assert the provider refetches `perspective.list` and the resulting `activePerspective.group` reflects the new value.
+- [ ] Existing tests still pass:
+  - All cases in `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/undo_commands.rs` — the generic UndoCmd stays functional for crates that don't mount perspectives.
+  - `swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/undo_cross_cutting.rs` — task/tag/column undo paths unchanged.
+  - `swissarmyhammer-perspectives/src/context.rs` existing tests including `setup_with_undo` coverage.
+- [ ] Run: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-perspectives -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` and `cd kanban-app/ui && bun test perspective-context` — all passing.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd`. Start with `perspective_group_undo_reverts_and_emits_event` in `undo_cross_cutting.rs` — it reproduces the user's bug end-to-end. Then add `reload_from_disk` + the `KanbanUndoCmd` wrapper to make it pass.
+- Do NOT modify the entity-level `UndoCmd`/`RedoCmd` in `swissarmyhammer-entity`. The crate ownership is correct: perspective reconciliation belongs in the kanban crate that knows about perspectives.
+- Do NOT change `PerspectiveContext::write` or `::delete` — the event emission on normal writes is already correct and must not fire twice.
+- Keep the existing `sync_entity_cache_from_disk` call in the wrapper — entity undo reconciliation is orthogonal and both hooks must run regardless of which store owned the undo target (the `store_name` discriminator routes each one).
\ No newline at end of file

 This is the same architectural shape as the earlier board-redraw-after-column-undo bug (`01KPTHBAXKGZGT9DA2E60KT8DK`), applied to perspectives instead of columns. The entity crate has a dedicated post-undo cache-sync hook; perspectives don't.

-**Confirmed by tracing**:
+## Approach (implemented)

-- `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/undo_commands.rs:44-111` — `UndoCmd::execute` calls `StoreContext::undo()` (which rewrites the file on disk), then calls `sync_entity_cache(ctx, &outcome)`, which only knows about `EntityContext::sync_entity_cache_from_disk`. Perspectives don't live in `EntityContext`.
-- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/mod.rs:256-257` — the generic `swissarmyhammer_entity::UndoCmd` and `RedoCmd` are registered directly as `app.undo` / `app.redo`. There's no kanban-level wrapper that also syncs perspective state.
-- `swissarmyhammer-perspectives/src/context.rs`:
-  - `PerspectiveContext::write` at lines 93-140 emits `PerspectiveEvent::PerspectiveChanged` broadcast (line 131-132) when mutations happen through `.write()`.
-  - **There is no path that emits that event when the file is rewritten by undo.** `PerspectiveStore::undo_erased` rewrites bytes on disk without going through `PerspectiveContext::write`.
-  - No `reload_from_disk` method exists on `PerspectiveContext`; nothing re-reads a single perspective's YAML after an external rewrite.
-- `kanban-app/src/watcher.rs:792-830` — `process_perspective_event` is the bridge that translates `PerspectiveEvent::PerspectiveChanged` into the `entity-field-changed` Tauri event the frontend `perspective-context.tsx` listens for. So IF the event fires, the frontend automatically reacts — the bridge is fine; the bug is that the event never fires on undo.
-
-## Approach
-
-Two parts: (1) teach `PerspectiveContext` to sync its cache and emit the event on post-undo refresh, (2) make `app.undo` / `app.redo` call it when the undo target was a perspective.

+```rust
+/// Refresh a single perspective's in-memory entry from disk.
+///
+/// Used by post-undo / post-redo reconciliation after the store layer has
+/// rewritten the on-disk YAML without going through `write()`. Mirrors the
+/// `EntityContext::sync_entity_cache_from_disk` contract at the entity layer:
+///
+/// - If the file exists and parses, replace the cached entry and emit
+///   `PerspectiveEvent::PerspectiveChanged` so downstream subscribers
+///   (Tauri bridge, frontend refresh) react.
+/// - If the file is absent (undo of a create, or redo of a delete),
+///   evict the cached entry and emit `PerspectiveEvent::PerspectiveDeleted`.
+pub async fn reload_from_disk(&mut self, id: &str) -> Result<()> {
+    let path = self.perspective_path(id);
+    if path.exists() {
+        let content = tokio::fs::read_to_string(&path).await?;
+        let p: Perspective = serde_yaml_ng::from_str(&content)?;
+        // existing id_index/perspectives replace logic — factor from `write`
+        self.replace_or_insert(p.clone());
+        let _ = self.event_sender.send(PerspectiveEvent::PerspectiveChanged {
+            id: p.id.clone(),
+            changed_fields: vec![],  // empty = "all fields may have changed"
+            is_create: false,
+        });
+    } else {
+        self.remove_by_id(id);
+        let _ = self.event_sender.send(PerspectiveEvent::PerspectiveDeleted { id: id.into() });
+    }
+    Ok(())
+}
+```

+```rust
+/// Refresh a single perspective's in-memory entry from disk.
+///
+/// Used by post-undo / post-redo reconciliation after the store layer has
+/// rewritten the on-disk YAML without going through `write()`. Mirrors the
+/// `EntityContext::sync_entity_cache_from_disk` contract at the entity layer:
+///
+/// - If the file exists and parses, replace the cached entry and emit
+///   `PerspectiveEvent::PerspectiveChanged` so downstream subscribers
+///   (Tauri bridge, frontend refresh) react.
+/// - If the file is absent (undo of a create, or redo of a delete),
+///   evict the cached entry and emit `PerspectiveEvent::PerspectiveDeleted`.
+pub async fn reload_from_disk(&mut self, id: &str) -> Result<()> {
+    let path = self.perspective_path(id);
+    if path.exists() {
+        let content = tokio::fs::read_to_string(&path).await?;
+        let p: Perspective = serde_yaml_ng::from_str(&content)?;
+        // existing id_index/perspectives replace logic — factor from `write`
+        self.replace_or_insert(p.clone());
+        let _ = self.event_sender.send(PerspectiveEvent::PerspectiveChanged {
+            id: p.id.clone(),
+            changed_fields: vec![],  // empty = "all fields may have changed"
+            is_create: false,
+        });
+    } else {
+        self.remove_by_id(id);
+        let _ = self.event_sender.send(PerspectiveEvent::PerspectiveDeleted { id: id.into() });
+    }
+    Ok(())
+}
+```
